free_text: Resonance strength measurements of the
27-Al(p,gamma)28-Si reaction in the energy range Ep =
Laboratory for material analysis.
The targets used during the whole set of measurements
were produced by evaporation of pure metallic
aluminium (99.9999%) on clean Cu and W backings.  In
each evaporation, close to the W and Cu backing a 2
mg/cm**2 gold foil was placed.  These foils were used
to determine the O and C present in the targets by
alpha RBS analysis at Ep= 3.06 MeV.  In all cases the
O/Al and C/Al atomic ratios were less than 0.006.  All
targets were free of fluorine.
The proton beam energy spread was found to be
1.6 +/- 0.2KeV at Ep=1.0 MeV.
See sample
The detection system used during the whole
experiment is a 4pi NaI(Tl) summing detector.
The absolute efficiency of this detection system has
been determined by calculations with MONTE-CARLO
simulation, based on the GEANT package.
Due to the fact that the final signal is the
sum of all the gamma-rays emitted, the absolute
efficiency depends strongly on the multiplicity and/or
the energy of the photons detected.  In the case of the
27-Al(p,g)28-Si reaction, the absolute efficiency was
calculated for each resonance separately taking into
account the specific decay scheme of each resonance.
Last checking has been done.
The letter to S.Harissopulos Has not any reply.
Reaction rate is compared with NACRE data.
Detailed analysis method is presented.
